paper_title: Analysis of Residual Hydrocarbons and Bacterial Species in Polar Sediments

methods.description: This method involves the extraction and analysis of residual hydrocarbons from sediment samples to monitor biodegradation efficiency using gas chromatography.

steps.description: Residual hydrocarbons from sediment subsamples (100 g) at the experiment beginning (0 days), after 90 days of incubation and end (160 days), were extracted using methyl-tertiary-butyl-ether:hexane (20:80).
steps.searchable_summary: extract hydrocarbons from sediment samples using solvent mixture

steps.description: The entire sample was transferred in a dark glass bottle, using sodium sulfate (5 g) as drying agent.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer sample to glass bottle with sodium sulfate as drying agent

steps.description: The liquid layer of solvent was poured through a funnel built with filter (Whatman N • 2) Na 2 SO 4 in a 250 mL pre-equilibrate evaporation flask.
steps.searchable_summary: filter solvent through sodium sulfate funnel into evaporation flask

steps.description: The extract was concentrated to a small volume by evaporation under reduced pressure in a rotavapor, and 1 mL was pipetted into glass vials with Teflon cap and refrigerated until analysis by gas chromatography.
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate extract using rotavapor and store in vials for GC analysis

steps.description: The characterization of petroleum product was made after solvent extraction methods followed by gas chromatography analysis with a Flame Ionization Detector (FID).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze hydrocarbon extract using gas chromatography with FID

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of dominant bacterial species in Arctic and Antarctic sediments using DGGE and 16S rRNA gene sequencing.

steps.description: A total of 77 bands were excised from gel and analyzed for taxonomical affiliation by 16S rRNA gene sequencing.
steps.searchable_summary: excise DGGE bands and perform 16S rRNA gene sequencing

steps.description: The phylogenetic analysis of partial 16S rRNA gene sequences from selected DGGE bands revealed bacterial affiliations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform phylogenetic analysis on 16S rRNA sequences

steps.description: Comparison of the Bray-Curtis dissimilarity matrices showed a similarity within microcosms communities.
steps.searchable_summary: compare community similarity using Bray-Curtis dissimilarity

steps.description: The nMDS analysis computed on DGGE matrix results showed different clustering of polar communities.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize community clustering using nMDS analysis
